item.text: I’ve got the helper shape now. I’m editing the asset-management and commit files next: one reusable builder for asset-to-clip mutations, and one inline reconciliation pass inside `materializeData` so deleted pinned clips are handled before commit.

I’ve moved from read-only analysis into the commit path now. The reconciliation helper is being written against the actual row/meta/clip-order data, so deleted pinned clips and deleted video toggles are resolved before `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` runs.

The mutation hook file is in place. I’m wiring shot-drop now by resolving the dragged shot through `ShotsContext`, registering each image asset, and batching the resulting clip/meta/order updates into one `applyEdit` with a pinned-group override.

I’m adding one focused drop test instead of broad new coverage: it verifies the actual contract here, which is “shot drop becomes one `rows` edit with a pinned-group override and never routes through `handleAssetDrop`.” After that I’ll add the commit-path reconciliation test to prove the deletion case stays on one commit boundary.

I’m adding one direct `useTimelineCommit` hook test instead of trying to infer this through unrelated callers. That gives us a clean check that reconciliation happens inside the commit path itself and doesn’t emit a second commit boundary when a pinned clip disappears.

The core edits are in. I’m running the full batch verification set now: serialize, shot-group hooks, the updated drop test module, the commit-path test module, then a full `npm run build` for type safety.

item.text: I’m making the inference layer aware of pinned groups now. The key constraint is preserving old behavior when no pinned data is passed, so inferred groups stay byte-for-byte the same and only pinned overlays add new shape.

The switch hook is being refactored around pinned-group state now. I’m snapshotting image clips before replacement, then using the existing `rows` mutation path with `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in both directions so undo/history stays coalesced to one edit per toggle.

The sync hook is the largest change in this batch, so I’m keeping it self-contained in `usePinnedShotGroups.ts`. It will debounce on editor/shot changes, compute desired image order from `ShotsContext`, and emit one `rows` edit with a pinned-group override if anything actually diverges.

I’m wiring the editor to the new pinned-group primitives now: pass persisted pinned groups into `useShotGroups`, swap the final-video hook to its bidirectional API, and start the debounced shot-image sync from the editor where both `shots` and `registerGenerationAsset` are already available.

I’m adding focused hook tests for the two risky behaviors in this batch: the switch hook’s snapshot/restore flow, and the auto-sync hook’s debounced `rows` edit. That gives us direct evidence for SC11 and SC15 instead of relying only on code inspection.

The hook and editor wiring edits are in. I’m running the focused modules that exercise the changed surfaces now: shot-group inference, the new switch/sync hook tests, the TimelineEditor utility tests, then a full build.
